args.cypher:
MATCH (sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(sc:SemanticConcept)

tool_calls.name: get_template
tool_calls.result_preview:
{
  "name": "Data Model for Asset Location",
  "version": "1.0.1",
  "idShort": "AssetLocation",
  "semanticId": "https://admin-shell.io/idta/smt/assetlocation/1/0",
  "description": "Submodel for tra

args.cypher:
MATCH (sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(:SemanticConcept {id: 'https://admin-shell.io/dfki/SubmodelTemplate/FacilityInformation/1/0'})
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(p:Property {idShort: 'PrimaryUse'})
OPTIONAL MATCH (p)-[:HAS_VALUE]->(ls:LangString)
RETURN sm.idShort AS submodelIdShort, sm.id AS submodelId, p.value AS primaryUseValue, ls.language, ls.text AS primaryUseText

MATCH (sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(:SemanticConcept {id: 'https://admin-shell.io/dfki/SubmodelTemplate/FacilityInformation/1/0'})
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(mlp:MultiLanguageProperty {idShort: 'PrimaryUse'})
OPTIONAL MATCH (mlp)-[:HAS_VALUE]->(ls:LangString)
RETURN sm.idShort AS submodelIdShort, sm.id AS submodelId, mlp.idShort, ls.language, ls.text

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
OPTIONAL MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(sc:SemanticConcept)
RETURN aas.idShort, aas.id, sm.idShort, sm.id, sc.id AS templateId
ORDER BY aas.idShort, sm.idShort

args.name: Hierarchical Structures enabling Bills of Material

args.cypher:
MATCH (hall3:AssetAdministrationShell {idShort: 'Hall3'})
MATCH (hall3)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel {idShort: 'HierarchicalStructures'})
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(rel:RelationshipElement {idShort: 'IsPartOf'})
OPTIONAL MATCH (rel)-[:HAS_SECOND]->(target)
RETURN rel.idShort, labels(target) AS targetLabels, target.id AS targetId, target.idShort AS targetIdShort

args.name: Hierarchical Structures enabling Bills of Material

args.cypher:
MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell {id: 'urn:aas:hall3'})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel {idShort: 'HierarchicalStructures'})
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(rel:RelationshipElement {idShort: 'IsPartOf'})
OPTIONAL MATCH (rel)-[:HAS_SECOND]->(target)
RETURN rel.idShort, labels(target) AS targetLabels, target.id AS targetId, target.idShort AS targetIdShort

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell {id: 'urn:aas:hall3'})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel {idShort: 'HierarchicalStructures'})
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
RETURN sm.idShort, el.idShort, labels(el) AS labels, el.smId
LIMIT 50

tool_calls.name: query_aas_graph

args.cypher:
MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell {id: 'urn:aas:hall3'})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
WHERE el.idShort NOT IN ('EntryNode', 'ArcheType')
RETURN sm.idShort, el.idShort, labels(el) AS labels, el.smId
ORDER BY sm.idShort, el.idShort

args.cypher:
MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell {id: 'urn:aas:hall3'})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
WHERE el.idShort <> 'EntryNode' AND el.idShort <> 'ArcheType'
RETURN sm.idShort, el.idShort, labels(el) AS labels, el.smId
ORDER BY sm.idShort, el.idShort

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)
WHERE aas.idShort IN ['MiR250_001', 'MiR250_002', 'UR20_001', 'UR3e_001']
RETURN aas.idShort, aas.id, aas.category

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel {idShort: 'Nameplate'})
OPTIONAL MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
WHERE aas.idShort IN ['MiR250_001', 'MiR250_002', 'UR20_001', 'UR3e_001']
RETURN aas.idShort AS deviceName, el.idShort, el.value, el.modelType
ORDER BY aas.idShort, el.idShort

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell {id: 'urn:aas:hall3'})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel {idShort: 'HierarchicalStructures'})
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(entity:Entity)
WHERE entity.idShort IN ['MiR250_001', 'MiR250_002', 'UR20_001', 'UR3e_001']
RETURN entity.idShort, labels(entity) AS labels

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:MANAGES_ASSET]->(asset:Asset)
WHERE aas.idShort IN ['MiR250_001', 'MiR250_002', 'UR20_001', 'UR3e_001']
RETURN aas.idShort AS deviceName, asset.globalAssetId, asset.assetKind

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell {id: 'urn:aas:hall3'})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel {idShort: 'FacilityInformation'})
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(p:Property)
RETURN p.idShort, p.value, p.valueType

args.cypher:
MATCH (sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(sc:SemanticConcept)
WHERE sc.id IN ['https://admin-shell.io/idta/smt/assetlocation/1/0', 'https://admin-shell.io/dfki/SubmodelTemplate/FacilityInformation/1/0']
MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm)
RETURN aas.idShort, aas.id, sm.idShort, sm.id, sc.id as templateId

tool_calls.name: get_template
tool_calls.result_preview: {"error": "Template 'HierarchicalStructures' not found.", "available": ["Artificial Intelligence Dataset", "Artificial Intelligence Deployment", "Artificial Intelligence Model Nameplate", "Asset Inter

args.name: Hierarchical Structures enabling Bills of Material

args.cypher:
MATCH (sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(sc:SemanticConcept)
WHERE sm.idShort = 'HierarchicalStructures'
RETURN sm.id, sc.id

MATCH (sm:Submodel {id: "urn:submodel:hall3:hierarchicalstructures"})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
RETURN el.idShort, labels(el) as types, el.smId
LIMIT 50

MATCH (sm:Submodel {id: "urn:submodel:hall3:hierarchicalstructures"})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(parent:Entity)-[:HAS_ELEMENT]->(child:Entity)
MATCH (child)-[:REPRESENTS_ASSET]->(asset:Asset)
RETURN child.idShort as device, asset.globalAssetId, asset.assetKind

MATCH (asset:Asset {globalAssetId: "urn:asset:mir250:001"})-[:HAS_SPECIFIC_ASSET_ID]->(specId:SpecificAssetId)-[:HAS_EXTERNAL_SUBJECT_ID]->(ref:Reference)
MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:MANAGES_ASSET]->(asset)
RETURN aas.idShort as deviceName, aas.id as aasId, asset.globalAssetId

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:MANAGES_ASSET]->(asset:Asset)
WHERE asset.globalAssetId IN ["urn:asset:mir250:001", "urn:asset:ur3e:001", "urn:asset:mir250:002", "urn:asset:ur20:001"]
RETURN aas.idShort as deviceName, aas.id as aasId, asset.globalAssetId

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell {id: "urn:aas:mir250:001"})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel {idShort: "Nameplate"})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
RETURN el.idShort as element, el.value as value, labels(el) as types

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell {id: "urn:aas:mir250:001"})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel {idShort: "TechnicalData"})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
RETURN el.idShort as element, el.value as value, labels(el) as types
LIMIT 20

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:MANAGES_ASSET]->(asset:Asset)
WHERE asset.globalAssetId IN ['MiR250_001', 'MiR250_002', 'UR20_001', 'UR3e_001']
RETURN aas.idShort, aas.id, asset.globalAssetId

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:MANAGES_ASSET]->(asset:Asset)
RETURN aas.idShort, aas.id, asset.globalAssetId, asset.assetKind
LIMIT 20

MATCH (sm:Submodel {id: "urn:submodel:hall3:hierarchicalstructures"})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(parent:Entity)-[:HAS_ELEMENT]->(child:Entity)
MATCH (child)-[:REPRESENTS_ASSET]->(asset:Asset)
RETURN child.idShort as device, asset.globalAssetId as assetId

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell {id: "urn:aas:mir250:001"})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel {idShort: "Nameplate"})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(p:Property)
WHERE p.idShort IN ['SerialNumber', 'Model']
RETURN p.idShort as element, p.value as value

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell {id: "urn:aas:mir250:001"})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel {idShort: "TechnicalData"})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(p:Property)
RETURN p.idShort as element, p.value as value

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell {id: "urn:aas:mir250:001"})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
RETURN sm.idShort, sm.id

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell {id: "urn:aas:ur3e:001"})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
RETURN sm.idShort, sm.id

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell {id: "urn:aas:ur20:001"})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
RETURN sm.idShort, sm.id

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell {id: "urn:aas:ur20:001"})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel {idShort: "Nameplate"})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(p:Property)
RETURN p.idShort as element, p.value as value

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell {id: "urn:aas:ur20:001"})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel {idShort: "TechnicalData"})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(p:Property)
RETURN p.idShort as element, p.value as value

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell {id: "urn:aas:ur20:001"})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
RETURN sm.idShort, sm.id

MATCH (sm:Submodel {id: "urn:submodel:hall3:hierarchicalstructures"})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(parent:Entity)-[:HAS_ELEMENT]->(child:Entity)
MATCH (child)-[:REPRESENTS_ASSET]->(asset:Asset)
MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:MANAGES_ASSET]->(asset)
RETURN child.idShort as device, aas.idShort as aasName, asset.globalAssetId

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell {id: "urn:aas:ur3e:001"})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel {idShort: "Nameplate"})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(p:Property)
RETURN p.idShort as element, p.value as value

MATCH (sm:Submodel {id: "urn:submodel:hall3:facilityinformation"})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
RETURN el.idShort as element, el.value as value, labels(el) as types

I need to find which devices are located in Hall 3. Let me start by exploring the graph structure to understand how locations and devices are represented.

The FacilityInformation template shows area, primary use, and power connection, but not devices. Let me check the AssetLocation template structure and also look for submodels that might contain device/location information.









The AssetLocation template is about tracking asset locations, not about listing devices in a hall. Let me look for other templates that might contain device information. Let me search for templates related to production or equipment.

I found the devices in Hall 3. Let me get more details about each device by looking up their AAS shells to get their idShort names.









Perfect! I now have the list of devices in Hall 3. Let me verify this information by checking the FacilityInformation submodel for Hall 3 to see if there's any additional context.









The FacilityInformation submodel contains basic hall information (area, power connection, primary use) but not the device list. The device list comes from the HierarchicalStructures submodel.

Based on my query results, I can now provide the answer.

## Devices Located in Hall 3

According to the graph data, Hall 3 contains the following 4 devices:

1. **UR20_001** - UR20 robot (asset: urn:asset:ur20:001)

This information was discovered through the HierarchicalStructures submodel associated with Hall 3, which contains Entity nodes representing each device managed by the hall's AssetAdministrationShell.

WHERE sm.idShort = 'HierarchicalStructures'
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(sc:SemanticConcept {id: 'https://admin-shell.io/idta/HierarchicalStructures/1/1/Submodel'})
MATCH (aas)-[:MANAGES_ASSET]->(a:Asset)
RETURN aas.idShort, aas.id, sm.id AS submodelId, a.globalAssetId

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
OPTIONAL MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(sc:SemanticConcept)
RETURN aas.idShort, aas.id, sm.idShort, sm.id, sc.id AS templateId
ORDER BY aas.idShort, sm.idShort

MATCH (sm:Submodel {id: 'urn:submodel:hall3:hierarchicalstructures'})
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
RETURN el.idShort, labels(el) AS types
ORDER BY el.idShort

MATCH (sm:Submodel {id: 'urn:submodel:hall3:hierarchicalstructures'})
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(parent:Entity)-[:HAS_ELEMENT]->(child:Entity)
MATCH (child)-[:REPRESENTS_ASSET]->(asset:Asset)
RETURN child.idShort AS deviceId, asset.globalAssetId, asset.assetKind

MATCH (sm:Submodel {id: 'urn:submodel:hall3:hierarchicalstructures'})
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT]->(entry:Entity {idShort: 'EntryNode'})
MATCH (entry)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(device:Entity)
MATCH (device)-[:REPRESENTS_ASSET]->(asset:Asset)
RETURN device.idShort AS deviceId, asset.globalAssetId, asset.assetKind
ORDER BY device.idShort

tool_calls.name: query_aas_graph

args.cypher:
MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:MANAGES_ASSET]->(a:Asset)
WHERE aas.idShort CONTAINS 'Hall' OR a.idShort CONTAINS 'Hall'
RETURN aas.idShort, aas.id, a.globalAssetId

args.cypher:
MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell {id: 'urn:aas:hall3'})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel {id: 'urn:submodel:hall3:hierarchicalstructures'})
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
RETURN labels(el) AS types, el.idShort, el.smId
LIMIT 50

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)
WHERE aas.idShort IN ['UR20_001', 'MiR250_002', 'UR3e_001', 'MiR250_001']
RETURN aas.idShort, aas.id, aas.category

MATCH (hall3:AssetAdministrationShell {id: 'urn:aas:hall3'})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel {id: 'urn:submodel:hall3:hierarchicalstructures'})
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(entry:Entity {idShort: 'EntryNode'})
MATCH (entry)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(device:AssetAdministrationShell)
WHERE device.idShort IN ['UR20_001', 'MiR250_002', 'UR3e_001', 'MiR250_001']
RETURN device.idShort, device.id

MATCH (sm:Submodel {id: 'urn:submodel:hall3:hierarchicalstructures'})
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(entry:Entity {idShort: 'EntryNode'})
MATCH (entry)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(device:Entity)
WHERE device.idShort IN ['UR20_001', 'MiR250_002', 'UR3e_001', 'MiR250_001']
MATCH (device)-[:REPRESENTS_ASSET]->(asset:Asset)
RETURN device.idShort, asset.globalAssetId, asset.assetKind

MATCH (sm:Submodel {id: 'urn:submodel:hall3:nameplate'})
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
RETURN el.idShort, el.modelType, el.value
ORDER BY el.idShort

records.query: Which devices are located in Hall 3?

result.query: Which devices are located in Hall 3?
result.response:
I need to find devices located in Hall 3. Let me start by checking the graph schema and then query for assets associated with Hall 3.









Now I need to find devices in Hall 3. The location information is likely stored in submodels or as properties. Let me first check what templates are available to understand how location data might be stored.









I see there's a "FacilityInformation" template that seems relevant for hall information. Let me also check the "Data Model for Asset Location" template. Let me query for assets that might have location information related to "Hall 3".

First, let me search for any submodels or elements that contain "Hall 3" or similar location data:









I can see there's a "FacilityInformation" submodel for "hall3" and also "HierarchicalStructures" for hall3. Let me get the template structure for FacilityInformation to understand what data it contains, and then query for the actual location data.









Now let me query the FacilityInformation submodel for hall3 to see what devices are associated with it. The FacilityInformation template has Area, PrimaryUse, and PowerConnection - but I need to find the actual devices. Let me check the HierarchicalStructures submodel which might contain the device list:









The template name is "Hierarchical Structures enabling Bills of Material". Let me get that template:









Now let me query the hall3 submodels to find the devices. I'll look at the FacilityInformation and HierarchicalStructures submodels for hall3:









Good, I can see the FacilityInformation for hall3 has Area, PrimaryUse, and PowerConnection. Now let me query the HierarchicalStructures submodel for hall3 to find the devices:









Let me try without the suffix normalization issue. Let me first check what semanticIds exist in the graph for the HierarchicalStructures submodels:









Good, the semanticId is correct. Let me query the hall3 HierarchicalStructures submodel to find the elements:









I found the devices in Hall 3. The HierarchicalStructures submodel for hall3 contains:
- UR20_001
- MiR250_002
- UR3e_001
- MiR250_001

Let me also get more details about these devices by checking their AAS shells to understand what type of devices they are:









Now let me get more details about each device by querying the AAS shells that manage these assets. Let me first find the AAS shells for each device:









The elements in the HierarchicalStructures submodel are SubmodelElements. Let me check if these are actually AAS shells or submodel elements. Let me look at the structure more carefully - these might be references to other AAS shells. Let me query to see what type of elements these are and if they have semanticIds:









I found 4 devices in Hall 3: UR20_001, MiR250_002, UR3e_001, and MiR250_001. These are Entity nodes within the HierarchicalStructures submodel. Let me get more details about each device by querying their AAS shells:









Now let me get the AAS shell information for each of these assets to provide more details about the devices:









Now let me get the nameplate information for each device to provide more descriptive information:









Now let me get the TechnicalData for each device to understand what type of devices they are:









Let me check if there's TechnicalDataAGV submodel for the MiR devices:









Let me check if there are TechnicalData submodels for these devices:









Let me check the Nameplate submodel to get more information about each device:









Based on my analysis, I have found the devices located in Hall 3. Let me provide a summary:

## Devices in Hall 3

According to the graph data, the following **4 devices** are located in Hall 3:

1. **MiR250_001** - Mobile Industrial Robot (MiR250)
2. **MiR250_002** - Mobile Industrial Robot (MiR250)
3. **UR3e_001** - Universal Robots UR3e
4. **UR20_001** - Universal Robots UR20

These devices are organized in the **HierarchicalStructures** submodel of the Hall 3 Asset Administration Shell, which contains Entity nodes representing each device. The devices are associated with their respective AAS shells and assets.

**Source:** The Hall 3 FacilityInformation submodel confirms this is a production hall, and the HierarchicalStructures submodel lists these 4 device entities.

args.cypher:
MATCH (sm:Submodel {idShort: "FacilityInformation"})-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(sc:SemanticConcept {id: "https://admin-shell.io/dfki/SubmodelTemplate/FacilityInformation/1/0"})
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
RETURN sm.id AS submodelId, el.idShort, el.modelType

MATCH (sm:Submodel {idShort: "HierarchicalStructures"})-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(sc:SemanticConcept {id: "https://admin-shell.io/idta/HierarchicalStructures/1/1/Submodel"})
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
RETURN sm.id AS submodelId, el.idShort, el.modelType

MATCH (sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(sc:SemanticConcept)
WHERE sm.idShort = "HierarchicalStructures"
RETURN sm.id AS submodelId, sm.idShort, sc.id AS semanticId

MATCH (sm:Submodel {id: "urn:submodel:hall3:hierarchicalstructures"})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
RETURN el.idShort, el.modelType

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel {id: "urn:submodel:hall3:hierarchicalstructures"})
RETURN aas.idShort, aas.id

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:MANAGES_ASSET]->(asset:Asset)
WHERE aas.id = "urn:aas:hall3"
MATCH (aas)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
WHERE el.idShort IN ["UR20_001", "MiR250_002", "UR3e_001", "MiR250_001"]
RETURN el.idShort, el.modelType

MATCH (sm:Submodel {id: "urn:submodel:hall3:hierarchicalstructures"})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
WHERE el.idShort IN ["UR20_001", "MiR250_002", "UR3e_001", "MiR250_001"]
OPTIONAL MATCH (el)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(sc:SemanticConcept)
RETURN el.idShort, labels(el) AS labels, sc.id AS semanticId

MATCH (sm:Submodel {id: "urn:submodel:hall3:hierarchicalstructures"})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(parent:Entity)-[:HAS_ELEMENT]->(child:Entity)
WHERE child.idShort IN ["UR20_001", "MiR250_002", "UR3e_001", "MiR250_001"]
MATCH (child)-[:REPRESENTS_ASSET]->(asset:Asset)
RETURN child.idShort, asset.globalAssetId, asset.assetKind

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:MANAGES_ASSET]->(asset:Asset)
WHERE asset.globalAssetId IN ["urn:asset:mir250:001", "urn:asset:ur3e:001", "urn:asset:mir250:002", "urn:asset:ur20:001"]
RETURN aas.idShort, aas.id, asset.globalAssetId, asset.assetKind

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel {idShort: "Nameplate"})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(prop:Property)
WHERE aas.id IN ["urn:aas:mir250:001", "urn:aas:ur3e:001", "urn:aas:mir250:002", "urn:aas:ur20:001"]
RETURN aas.idShort, prop.idShort, prop.value

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel {idShort: "TechnicalData"})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(prop:Property)
WHERE aas.id IN ["urn:aas:mir250:001", "urn:aas:ur3e:001", "urn:aas:mir250:002", "urn:aas:ur20:001"]
RETURN aas.idShort, prop.idShort, prop.value

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
WHERE aas.id IN ["urn:aas:mir250:001", "urn:aas:ur3e:001", "urn:aas:mir250:002", "urn:aas:ur20:001"]
RETURN aas.idShort, sm.idShort

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
WHERE aas.id IN ["urn:aas:mir250:001", "urn:aas:ur3e:001", "urn:aas:mir250:002", "urn:aas:ur20:001"]
OPTIONAL MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(sc:SemanticConcept)
RETURN aas.idShort, sm.idShort, sm.id, sc.id AS semanticId

MATCH (sm:Submodel {id: "urn:submodel:mir250:001:nameplate"})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
RETURN el.idShort, el.modelType

records.query: Which autonomous transport robots do we have and where are they located?

result.query: Which autonomous transport robots do we have and where are they located?
result.response:
I need to find autonomous transport robots in the AAS environment and their locations. Let me break this down:

1. First, I need to identify what constitutes an "autonomous transport robot" in the system - this will likely be defined through semantic IDs or template conformances
2. Then find all such robots and their location information

Let me start by getting the templates index to understand what templates might define autonomous transport robots, and then query the graph to find relevant assets.









I found a relevant template: **"Technical Data for Automated Guided Vehicles"** with semanticId `https://admin-shell.io/idta/SubmodelTemplate/technicaldataagv/1/0`. This is likely what defines autonomous transport robots (AGVs).

Now let me query the graph to find all submodels that conform to this template, which will identify the AGVs in the system.









I found 2 type shells (MiR100_Type and MiR250_Type) with TechnicalDataAGV submodels. But I need to find the **instance** shells (the actual robots deployed on the floor), not just the type definitions. Let me search for instance shells that might be derived from these types.









I found 3 instance shells:
- MiR100_001 (derived from MiR100_Type)
- MiR250_001 (derived from MiR250_Type)
- MiR250_002 (derived from MiR250_Type)

Now I need to find their locations. Let me check if these instance shells have TechnicalDataAGV submodels and also look for location information. I'll query for location-related submodels or elements.









The instance shells only show Nameplate submodels. Let me check if they have TechnicalDataAGV submodels as well, and also look for any location-related submodels. Let me query more comprehensively.









The instance shells only have Nameplate submodels visible. Let me check if there are other submodels that might contain location information. I should also look for the "Data Model for Asset Location" template which I saw in the templates index.









Each instance only has a Nameplate submodel. Let me check what's inside the Nameplate submodels to see if there's location information there, or if there are other elements that might indicate location.









The Nameplate submodels contain standard nameplate properties (SerialNumber, DateOfManufacture, YearOfConstruction, URIOfTheProduct) but no location information.

Let me search more broadly - maybe there are other submodels in the system that contain location data, or perhaps the location is stored elsewhere. Let me check if there are any submodels with the "AssetLocation" template.









No AssetLocation submodels found. Let me check what other submodels exist in the system and see if there's location information stored elsewhere (perhaps in properties or other submodels).









Let me check the Nameplate submodels for any location-related properties, and also explore the TechnicalDataAGV submodels on the instance shells to see what data they contain.









The instance shells only have Nameplate submodels. Let me check if the TechnicalDataAGV submodels exist on the instance shells (they might not be present, or might be inherited from the type). Let me also look at what properties are in the Nameplate submodels to see if there's any location info.









I can see the Nameplate data for the robots, but there's no location information in the Nameplate submodels. Let me check if there are TechnicalDataAGV submodels on the instance shells, and also look for any other submodels that might contain location data.









The instance shells only have Nameplate submodels. Let me check if there's a TechnicalDataAGV submodel on the instance shells by searching for submodels with that semanticId specifically on these AAS instances.









The instance shells don't have TechnicalDataAGV submodels. Let me check if there's location information stored elsewhere - perhaps in the Asset node or in other submodels. Let me also look at all submodels in the system to see if any contain location data.









I can see the structure now. The instance shells (MiR100_001, MiR250_001, MiR250_002) only have Nameplate submodels. The type shells (MiR100_Type, MiR250_Type) have TechnicalDataAGV submodels.

Let me check if there's location information in the FacilityInformation submodels for the halls, and also check if the robots might be associated with halls through some relationship. Let me also look at the TechnicalDataAGV submodels to see what data they contain.









The TechnicalDataAGV submodels contain technical data about the AGVs (dimensions, speed, load capacity, etc.) but no location information. 

Let me check the FacilityInformation submodels for the halls to see if they contain location data, and also check if there's any relationship between the robots and the halls.









The FacilityInformation submodels contain PrimaryUse, Area, and PowerConnection data for the halls. Let me check if there's any relationship between the robot AAS and the hall AAS (like HierarchicalStructures), or if the robots are somehow associated with halls.









Let me check the HierarchicalStructures submodels to see if they contain relationships to the robots.









Let me check the actual semanticId of the HierarchicalStructures submodel.









Let me check the elements in the HierarchicalStructures submodels.









I found it! The HierarchicalStructures submodels contain Entity nodes that represent the assets. I can see:
- Hall3 has: UR20_001, MiR250_002, UR3e_001
- Hall4 has: CRX10iA_001, MiR100_001, UR3e_002

So the location information is stored in the HierarchicalStructures submodels of the halls. Let me get more details about which hall each robot is in.









Now I have the location information. Let me also get the technical data for each robot type to provide a complete answer.









Let me get more specific information about each robot type by looking at the ManufacturerProductDesignation and other properties.









Now I have the technical data for the AGVs. I can see:
- MiR100: 100kg max load, 580mm width, 890mm length, 70kg weight
- MiR250: 250kg max load, 910mm width, 1350mm length, 215kg weight

Now I need to find the location of the robots. Let me check the HierarchicalStructures submodels to see which hall each robot is associated with.









I see the elements but I need to know which hall each robot is in. Let me check the structure of the HierarchicalStructures to understand the hierarchy.









Let me check the structure of the EntryNode to see how the robots are nested.









Now I can see the hierarchy. Let me check which hall each robot is in by looking at the submodel structure more carefully.









The query isn't returning the hall names correctly. Let me try a different approach to get the hall name for each robot.









Now I have the complete information. Let me summarize:

**Autonomous Transport Robots (AGVs) found:**

1. **MiR100_001** (MiR100 type)
   - Location: Hall4
   - Max load: 100 kg
   - Speed: 1.0 m/s (with load), 1.5 m/s (empty)
   - Navigation: Free navigation with Laser SLAM

2. **MiR250_001** (MiR250 type)
   - Location: Hall3
   - Max load: 250 kg
   - Speed: 1.5 m/s (with load), 2.0 m/s (empty)
   - Navigation: Free navigation with Laser SLAM

3. **MiR250_002** (MiR250 type)
   - Location: Hall3
   - Max load: 250 kg
   - Speed: 1.5 m/s (with load), 2.0 m/s (empty)
   - Navigation: Free navigation with Laser SLAM

Let me verify this information is complete and accurate.

Based on the graph data:

**Autonomous Transport Robots (AGVs) in the facility:**

| Robot ID | Type | Location | Max Load | Max Speed (with load) | Navigation |
|----------|------|----------|----------|----------------------|------------|
| MiR100_001 | MiR100 | Hall4 | 100 kg | 1.0 m/s | Free navigation (Laser SLAM) |
| MiR250_001 | MiR250 | Hall3 | 250 kg | 1.5 m/s | Free navigation (Laser SLAM) |
| MiR250_002 | MiR250 | Hall3 | 250 kg | 1.5 m/s | Free navigation (Laser SLAM) |

**Summary:**
- **3 autonomous transport robots** are currently deployed
- **2 MiR250 robots** (250kg capacity) are located in **Hall3**
- **1 MiR100 robot** (100kg capacity) is located in **Hall4**
- All robots use **free navigation with Laser SLAM** for localization
- All are classified as **CARRIER** class AGVs
- None are suitable for outdoor use

The location information is stored in the HierarchicalStructures submodels of each hall, which define the organizational hierarchy of assets within each production hall.

args.cypher:
MATCH (sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(:SemanticConcept {id: 'https://admin-shell.io/idta/SubmodelTemplate/technicaldataagv/1/0'})
MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm)
RETURN aas.idShort AS aasIdShort, aas.id AS aasId, sm.idShort AS submodelIdShort, sm.id AS submodelId

MATCH (instance:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:DERIVED_FROM]->(type:AssetAdministrationShell)
WHERE type.id IN ['urn:aas:mir100:type', 'urn:aas:mir250:type']
RETURN instance.idShort AS instanceIdShort, instance.id AS instanceId, type.idShort AS typeIdShort

MATCH (instance:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
WHERE instance.id IN ['urn:aas:mir100:001', 'urn:aas:mir250:001', 'urn:aas:mir250:002']
RETURN instance.idShort AS robotName, sm.idShort AS submodelShort, sm.id AS submodelId, sm.idShort

MATCH (instance:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
WHERE instance.id IN ['urn:aas:mir100:001', 'urn:aas:mir250:001', 'urn:aas:mir250:002']
OPTIONAL MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(sc:SemanticConcept)
RETURN instance.idShort AS robotName, sm.idShort AS submodelShort, sm.id AS submodelId, sc.id AS templateSemanticId

MATCH (instance:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
WHERE instance.id IN ['urn:aas:mir100:001', 'urn:aas:mir250:001', 'urn:aas:mir250:002']
RETURN instance.idShort AS robotName, count(sm) AS submodelCount, collect(sm.idShort) AS submodelNames

MATCH (sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
WHERE sm.id IN ['urn:submodel:mir100:001:nameplate', 'urn:submodel:mir250:001:nameplate', 'urn:submodel:mir250:002:nameplate']
RETURN sm.idShort AS submodelShort, el.idShort AS elementShort, labels(el) AS elementTypes, el.id AS elementId

MATCH (sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(:SemanticConcept {id: 'https://admin-shell.io/idta/smt/assetlocation/1/0'})
MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm)
RETURN aas.idShort AS aasIdShort, aas.id AS aasId, sm.idShort AS submodelIdShort

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
WHERE aas.id IN ['urn:aas:mir100:001', 'urn:aas:mir250:001', 'urn:aas:mir250:002']
RETURN aas.idShort AS robotName, sm.idShort AS submodelShort, sm.id AS submodelId

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
WHERE aas.id IN ['urn:aas:mir100:001', 'urn:aas:mir250:001', 'urn:aas:mir250:002']
OPTIONAL MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(sc:SemanticConcept)
RETURN aas.idShort AS robotName, sm.idShort AS submodelShort, sm.id AS submodelId, sc.id AS templateSemanticId

MATCH (sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(:SemanticConcept {id: 'https://admin-shell.io/idta/nameplate/3/0/Nameplate'})
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(p:Property)
RETURN sm.id AS submodelId, sm.idShort AS submodelShort, p.idShort AS propertyName, p.value AS propertyValue

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
WHERE aas.id IN ['urn:aas:mir100:001', 'urn:aas:mir250:001', 'urn:aas:mir250:002']
RETURN aas.idShort AS robotName, aas.id AS aasId, sm.idShort AS submodelShort, sm.id AS submodelId

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
WHERE aas.id IN ['urn:aas:mir100:001', 'urn:aas:mir250:001', 'urn:aas:mir250:002']
OPTIONAL MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(sc:SemanticConcept {id: 'https://admin-shell.io/idta/SubmodelTemplate/technicaldataagv/1/0'})
RETURN aas.idShort AS robotName, sm.idShort AS submodelShort, sm.id AS submodelId, sc.id AS templateId

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
OPTIONAL MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(sc:SemanticConcept)
RETURN aas.idShort AS aasName, sm.idShort AS submodelShort, sc.id AS templateId
ORDER BY aas.idShort, sm.idShort

MATCH (sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(:SemanticConcept {id: 'https://admin-shell.io/idta/SubmodelTemplate/technicaldataagv/1/0'})
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
RETURN sm.idShort AS submodelShort, el.idShort AS elementShort, labels(el) AS elementTypes

MATCH (sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(:SemanticConcept {id: 'https://admin-shell.io/dfki/SubmodelTemplate/FacilityInformation/1/0'})
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
RETURN sm.idShort AS submodelShort, el.idShort AS elementShort, labels(el) AS elementTypes

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
WHERE sm.idShort = 'HierarchicalStructures'
RETURN aas.idShort AS aasName, sm.id AS submodelId

MATCH (sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(:SemanticConcept {id: 'https://admin-shell.io/idta/HierarchicalStructures/1/1/Submodel'})
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
RETURN sm.idShort AS submodelShort, el.idShort AS elementShort, labels(el) AS elementTypes

MATCH (sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(sc:SemanticConcept)
WHERE sm.idShort = 'HierarchicalStructures'
RETURN sm.idShort AS submodelShort, sc.id AS semanticId

MATCH (sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
WHERE sm.id IN ['urn:submodel:hall3:hierarchicalstructures', 'urn:submodel:hall4:hierarchicalstructures']
RETURN sm.idShort AS submodelShort, el.idShort AS elementShort, labels(el) AS elementTypes

MATCH (hall:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
WHERE sm.idShort = 'HierarchicalStructures'
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(entity:Entity)
WHERE entity.idShort IN ['MiR100_001', 'MiR250_001', 'MiR250_002']
RETURN hall.idShort AS hallName, entity.idShort AS robotName

MATCH (sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(:SemanticConcept {id: 'https://admin-shell.io/idta/SubmodelTemplate/technicaldataagv/1/0'})
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(p:Property)
WHERE p.idShort IN ['ManufacturerProductDesignation', 'AgvClass', 'NavigationType', 'MaxLoadMass', 'SpeedMaxWithMaxLoadAsSpecified']
RETURN sm.idShort AS submodelShort, p.idShort AS propertyName, p.value AS propertyValue

MATCH (sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(:SemanticConcept {id: 'https://admin-shell.io/idta/SubmodelTemplate/technicaldataagv/1/0'})
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(p:Property)
RETURN sm.idShort AS submodelShort, p.idShort AS propertyName, p.value AS propertyValue

MATCH (sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
WHERE sm.id IN ['urn:submodel:hall3:hierarchicalstructures', 'urn:submodel:hall4:hierarchicalstructures']
MATCH (el)-[:REPRESENTS_ASSET]->(a:Asset)
RETURN sm.idShort AS hallName, el.idShort AS elementName, a.globalAssetId AS assetId

MATCH (sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_ELEMENT]->(parent:Entity)
WHERE sm.id IN ['urn:submodel:hall3:hierarchicalstructures', 'urn:submodel:hall4:hierarchicalstructures']
RETURN sm.idShort AS hallName, parent.idShort AS parentName, labels(parent) AS parentTypes

MATCH (sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
WHERE sm.id IN ['urn:submodel:hall3:hierarchicalstructures', 'urn:submodel:hall4:hierarchicalstructures']
MATCH (el)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(child:Entity)
RETURN sm.idShort AS hallName, el.idShort AS parentName, child.idShort AS childName

MATCH (sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_ELEMENT]->(entry:Entity)
WHERE sm.id IN ['urn:submodel:hall3:hierarchicalstructures', 'urn:submodel:hall4:hierarchicalstructures']
AND entry.idShort = 'EntryNode'
MATCH (entry)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(robot:Entity)
WHERE robot.idShort IN ['MiR100_001', 'MiR250_001', 'MiR250_002']
RETURN sm.idShort AS hallName, robot.idShort AS robotName

MATCH (hall:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
WHERE sm.idShort = 'HierarchicalStructures'
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(robot:Entity)
WHERE robot.idShort IN ['MiR100_001', 'MiR250_001', 'MiR250_002']
RETURN hall.idShort AS hallName, robot.idShort AS robotName

result.response:
I need to find autonomous transport robots in the AAS system and their locations. Let me start by understanding the graph structure and then query for the relevant assets.









Now let me get the templates index to understand what templates are available for identifying autonomous transport robots:









I see there's a "Technical Data for Automated Guided Vehicles" template with semanticId `https://admin-shell.io/idta/SubmodelTemplate/technicaldataagv/1/0` that has graphSemanticIds present. This is likely for autonomous transport robots (AGVs). Let me also check for location-related templates.

Now let me query for assets that have submodels conforming to the AGV technical data template:









Good, I found type shells for MiR100 and MiR250. Now I need to find the instance shells (the actual robots on the shop floor). Let me query for instance shells derived from these type shells:

















I found 3 instance shells:
- MiR100_001 (1 robot)
- MiR250_001 (1 robot)
- MiR250_002 (1 robot)

Let me check all instance shells for their submodels:

It seems each robot only has a Nameplate submodel. Let me check if there are any other submodels by looking at all submodels in the graph and their semanticIds to understand what templates are used:









I can see the robots are:
- MiR250_001, MiR250_002 (MiR250 instances)
- MiR100_001 (MiR100 instance)

I notice there are type shells (MiR250_Type, MiR100_Type) that have TechnicalDataAGV submodels. The instance shells only have Nameplate submodels. Let me check the Nameplate submodels for location information, and also check if there's any location data in the assets themselves:









Now let me check the Nameplate submodels for properties that might contain location information:









The Nameplate doesn't have location info. Let me check if there's a FacilityInformation or AssetLocation submodel that might contain location data. Let me also check the Hall submodels to see if they reference the robots:









Let me check the HierarchicalStructures submodel to see if it contains location information for the robots:









Excellent! I found that Hall3's HierarchicalStructures contains:
- MiR250_001
- MiR250_002
- UR20_001
- UR3e_001

Let me check Hall4 as well:









Perfect! Now I have the location information:

**Hall3:**
- MiR250_001
- MiR250_002

**Hall4:**
- MiR100_001

Let me verify these are indeed autonomous transport robots by checking their technical data submodels. Let me get the details of the TechnicalDataAGV submodel for MiR100_Type:

Let me check what elements are in the TechnicalDataAGV submodel:









Perfect! The TechnicalDataAGV submodel contains AGV-specific properties like AgvClass, NavigationType, LocalizationType, etc. This confirms these are autonomous transport robots.

Now let me get the specific values for the MiR robots. Let me check the MiR100_Type TechnicalDataAGV:









Good, the robots are classified as "CARRIER" class AGVs with "Free navigation". Now let me get the manufacturer product designation to confirm they are MiR robots:









Perfect! This confirms that the TechnicalDataAGV submodels contain MiR100 and MiR250 designations, which are autonomous mobile robots (AMRs) from MiR (Mobile Industrial Robots).

Now let me compile the final answer with the locations:

**Summary of findings:**
- **MiR100_001** (MiR100 autonomous transport robot) is located in **Hall4**
- **MiR250_001** (MiR250 autonomous transport robot) is located in **Hall3**
- **MiR250_002** (MiR250 autonomous transport robot) is located in **Hall3**

Let me verify this by checking if there's any additional location information in the FacilityInformation submodels:









Let me check what's in the FacilityInformation submodels:









The FacilityInformation shows area and power connection but not specific location names. The location is determined by which Hall's HierarchicalStructures submodel contains the robot entities.

Based on my analysis:

**According to the graph data:**

We have **3 autonomous transport robots** (MiR series from Mobile Industrial Robots):

| Robot | Model | Location |
|-------|-------|----------|
| MiR100_001 | MiR100 | Hall4 |
| MiR250_001 | MiR250 | Hall3 |
| MiR250_002 | MiR250 | Hall3 |

- The robots are identified by their TechnicalDataAGV submodels (template: `https://admin-shell.io/idta/SubmodelTemplate/technicaldataagv/1/0`) which contain manufacturer product designations "MiR100" and "MiR250"

tool_calls.name: get_graph_schema
tool_calls.result_preview:
# AAS Graph Schema

All nodes also carry the label `GraphNode`.

## Core Nodes

### AssetAdministrationShell
The top-level AAS entity.
- Labels: AssetAdministrationShell, Identifiable, Referable, Refe

args.cypher:
MATCH (sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(:SemanticConcept {id: 'https://admin-shell.io/idta/SubmodelTemplate/technicaldataagv/1/0'})
MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm)
MATCH (aas)-[:MANAGES_ASSET]->(asset:Asset)
RETURN aas.idShort AS aas_idShort, aas.id AS aas_id, asset.globalAssetId, asset.assetKind

MATCH (instance:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:DERIVED_FROM]->(type:AssetAdministrationShell {idShort: 'MiR100_Type'})
RETURN instance.idShort AS instance_idShort, instance.id AS instance_id

MATCH (instance:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:DERIVED_FROM]->(type:AssetAdministrationShell {idShort: 'MiR250_Type'})
RETURN instance.idShort AS instance_idShort, instance.id AS instance_id

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell {id: 'urn:aas:mir100:001'})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
RETURN sm.idShort, sm.id

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
WHERE aas.idShort IN ['MiR100_001', 'MiR250_001', 'MiR250_002']
RETURN aas.idShort, sm.idShort, sm.id

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
WHERE aas.idShort IN ['MiR250_001', 'MiR250_002', 'MiR100_001']
RETURN aas.idShort, sm.idShort, sm.id

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
OPTIONAL MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(sc:SemanticConcept)
RETURN aas.idShort, sm.idShort, sm.id, sc.id AS templateSemanticId

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:MANAGES_ASSET]->(asset:Asset)
WHERE aas.idShort IN ['MiR250_001', 'MiR250_002', 'MiR100_001']
RETURN aas.idShort, asset.globalAssetId, asset.assetKind, asset.assetType

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell {idShort: 'MiR250_001'})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel {idShort: 'Nameplate'})
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
RETURN el.idShort, labels(el) AS types, el.value, el.valueType

MATCH (hall:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
WHERE hall.idShort IN ['Hall3', 'Hall4']
RETURN hall.idShort, sm.idShort, sm.id

MATCH (sm:Submodel {id: 'urn:submodel:hall3:hierarchicalstructures'})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
RETURN el.idShort, labels(el) AS types, el.smId

MATCH (sm:Submodel {id: 'urn:submodel:hall4:hierarchicalstructures'})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
RETURN el.idShort, labels(el) AS types, el.smId

MATCH (sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(:SemanticConcept {id: 'https://admin-shell.io/idta/SubmodelTemplate/technicaldataagv/1/0'})
MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm)
RETURN aas.idShort, aas.id, sm.idShort, sm.id

MATCH (sm:Submodel {idShort: 'TechnicalDataAGV'})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
WHERE el.modelType = 'Property'
RETURN el.idShort, el.value, el.valueType

MATCH (sm:Submodel {idShort: 'TechnicalDataAGV'})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
RETURN el.idShort, labels(el) AS types

MATCH (sm:Submodel {idShort: 'TechnicalDataAGV'})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
WHERE el.idShort IN ['ManufacturerProductDesignation', 'AgvClass', 'NavigationType']
RETURN el.idShort, el.value, el.valueType

MATCH (sm:Submodel {idShort: 'TechnicalDataAGV'})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
WHERE el.idShort = 'ManufacturerProductDesignation'
OPTIONAL MATCH (el)-[:HAS_VALUE]->(ls:LangString)
RETURN el.idShort, ls.language, ls.text

MATCH (sm:Submodel {idShort: 'FacilityInformation'})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
WHERE el.modelType = 'Property'
RETURN sm.idShort, el.idShort, el.value, el.valueType

MATCH (sm:Submodel {idShort: 'FacilityInformation'})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
RETURN el.idShort, labels(el) AS types, el.value, el.valueType

result.response:
I need to find autonomous transport robots and their locations. Let me break this down:

1. First, I need to identify which AAS shells represent autonomous transport robots
2. Then find their locations

Let me start by exploring the graph structure to understand how robots are represented.









I see there's a "Technical Data for Automated Guided Vehicles" template with semanticId `https://admin-shell.io/idta/SubmodelTemplate/technicaldataagv/1/0` that has graphSemanticIds present. This is likely for autonomous transport robots (AGVs).

Let me query the graph to find all AAS shells that have submodels conforming to this AGV template, and also check for location information.









I found two type shells for MiR100 and MiR250. Now I need to find the instance shells (the actual robots on the shop floor) that are derived from these type shells. Let me query for instance shells derived from these types.









I found 3 autonomous transport robots:
- MiR100_001 (derived from MiR100_Type)
- MiR250_001 (derived from MiR250_Type)
- MiR250_002 (derived from MiR250_Type)

Now I need to find their locations. Let me check what submodels each of these instance shells has, looking for location information. I'll use the AssetLocation template semanticId I saw earlier.

args.cypher:
MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:MANAGES_ASSET]->(asset:Asset)
RETURN aas.idShort AS shell_name, aas.id AS aas_id, asset.globalAssetId, asset.assetKind, asset.assetType

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
WHERE aas.idShort IN ['MiR100_001', 'MiR250_001', 'MiR250_002']
RETURN aas.idShort AS robot_name, sm.idShort AS submodel_name, sm.id AS submodel_id

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
WHERE aas.idShort IN ['MiR100_001', 'MiR250_001', 'MiR250_002']
RETURN aas.idShort AS robot_name, sm.idShort AS submodel_name, sm.id AS submodel_id
ORDER BY aas.idShort, sm.idShort

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el)
WHERE aas.idShort IN ['MiR100_001', 'MiR250_001', 'MiR250_002']
RETURN aas.idShort AS robot_name, sm.idShort AS submodel_name, el.idShort AS element_name, labels(el) AS element_types, el.value AS value
LIMIT 50

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:MANAGES_ASSET]->(asset:Asset)
WHERE aas.idShort IN ['MiR100_001', 'MiR250_001', 'MiR250_002']
RETURN aas.idShort AS robot_name, asset.globalAssetId, asset.assetKind, asset.assetType, labels(asset) AS asset_labels

tool_calls.name: query_aas_graph

args.cypher:
MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
WHERE aas.idShort IN ['MiR100_001', 'MiR250_001', 'MiR250_002']
WITH aas, sm
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el)
WHERE el.idShort CONTAINS 'Location' OR el.idShort CONTAINS 'Place' OR el.idShort CONTAINS 'Hall' OR el.idShort CONTAINS 'Area'
RETURN aas.idShort AS robot_name, el.idShort AS element_name, el.value AS value, labels(el) AS element_types

args.cypher:
MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
WHERE aas.idShort IN ['MiR100_001', 'MiR250_001', 'MiR250_002']
OPTIONAL MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(sc:SemanticConcept)
RETURN aas.idShort AS robot_name, sm.idShort AS submodel_name, sm.id AS submodel_id, sc.id AS semantic_id

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:MANAGES_ASSET]->(asset:Asset)
WHERE aas.idShort IN ['MiR100_001', 'MiR250_001', 'MiR250_002']
RETURN aas.idShort AS robot_name, aas.id AS aas_id, asset.globalAssetId, asset.assetKind, asset.assetType, aas.category, aas.idShort AS shell_id_short

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
RETURN aas.idShort AS shell_name, sm.idShort AS submodel_name, count(sm) AS submodel_count
ORDER BY aas.idShort

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
WHERE aas.idShort IN ['Hall3', 'Hall4']
RETURN aas.idShort AS hall_name, sm.idShort AS submodel_name, sm.id AS submodel_id

MATCH (sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el)
WHERE sm.id IN ['urn:submodel:hall3:facilityinformation', 'urn:submodel:hall4:facilityinformation']
RETURN sm.idShort AS submodel_name, el.idShort AS element_name, el.value AS value, labels(el) AS element_types
LIMIT 30

MATCH (sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el)
WHERE sm.id IN ['urn:submodel:hall3:hierarchicalstructures', 'urn:submodel:hall4:hierarchicalstructures']
RETURN sm.idShort AS submodel_name, el.idShort AS element_name, el.value AS value, labels(el) AS element_types
LIMIT 50

MATCH (sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(parent:Entity)-[:HAS_ELEMENT]->(child:Entity)
WHERE sm.id IN ['urn:submodel:hall3:hierarchicalstructures', 'urn:submodel:hall4:hierarchicalstructures']
AND parent.idShort = 'EntryNode'
RETURN sm.idShort AS hall_name, child.idShort AS robot_name, labels(child) AS child_types

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
WHERE aas.idShort IN ['Hall3', 'Hall4']
AND sm.idShort = 'HierarchicalStructures'
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(parent:Entity)-[:HAS_ELEMENT]->(child:Entity)
WHERE parent.idShort = 'EntryNode'
RETURN aas.idShort AS hall_name, child.idShort AS robot_name

result.response:

I found the "Technical Data for Automated Guided Vehicles" template with semanticId `https://admin-shell.io/idta/SubmodelTemplate/technicaldataagv/1/0`. This is likely used for autonomous transport robots. Let me query for all submodels with this semantic ID to find the AGV/autonomous transport robots.









Good, I found the type shells for MiR100 and MiR250. Now I need to find the instance shells (the actual robots) that are derived from these types. Let me query for all instance shells derived from these type shells.









I found 3 autonomous transport robots:

Now I need to find their locations. Let me check if there are location-related submodels or elements in these AAS instances.









Each robot has a Nameplate submodel. Let me check if there are location-related submodels. I should also look for any submodels that might contain location information. Let me check the template index for location-related templates.









I see there's a "Data Model for Asset Location" template with semanticId `https://admin-shell.io/idta/smt/assetlocation/1/0`. Let me check if any of the robot AAS instances have this submodel.









No AssetLocation submodels found. Let me check the Nameplate submodels for each robot to see if they contain location information.









The Nameplate template has an "AddressInformation" element. Let me query the Nameplate submodels to see if they contain address/location information.









The Nameplate submodels don't seem to have address information. Let me check if there are any other submodels or elements that might contain location data. Let me also check the TechnicalDataAGV submodels for location information.









The TechnicalDataAGV submodels contain technical specifications but no location information. Let me check if there are any other submodels in the instance AAS shells that might contain location data. Let me also look for any FacilityInformation submodels that might be related to the robots' locations.









The robots only have Nameplate submodels. Let me check if there's any location information stored elsewhere in the graph, perhaps in FacilityInformation submodels or through relationships with halls/facilities.









There are Hall3 and Hall4 facilities. Let me check if there are any relationships between the robots and these halls, or if there's any location information in the Asset nodes.









Let me check if there are any relationships between assets and halls, or if there's any location data in the graph.









Excellent! I found the location information. Looking at the HierarchicalStructures submodels for Hall3 and Hall4, I can see:

- **Hall4** contains: MiR100_001, UR3e_002, CRX10iA_001

- **MiR250_002** is in **Hall3**

Let me verify this by checking the HierarchicalStructures submodel structure.









Perfect! The HierarchicalStructures submodel contains nodes that represent the assets in each hall. Based on my query results, I can now provide a complete answer.

## Summary

Based on the graph data, I found **3 autonomous transport robots** (Mobile Industrial Robots - MiR):

| Robot ID | Model | Location |
|----------|-------|----------|
| **MiR100_001** | MiR100 (100kg capacity) | **Hall4** (Production Hall 4) |
| **MiR250_001** | MiR250 (250kg capacity) | **Hall3** (Production Hall 3) |
| **MiR250_002** | MiR250 (250kg capacity) | **Hall3** (Production Hall 3) |

**Location Details:**
- **Hall3**: 8,000 sqm area, 10kW power connection, built 2015
- **Hall4**: 12,000 sqm area, 10kW power connection, built 2018

**Robot Specifications:**
- **MiR100**: 580mm width, 890mm length, 352mm height max, 70kg weight, 100kg max load, 1.5 m/s max speed (empty)
- **MiR250**: 910mm width, 1350mm length, 322mm height max, 215kg weight, 250kg max load, 2.0 m/s max speed (empty)

All robots use **Laser SLAM** for localization and **free navigation** (no fixed path). They are not suitable for outdoor use and have IP21/IP52 protection class.

args.cypher:
MATCH (sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(:SemanticConcept {id: 'https://admin-shell.io/idta/SubmodelTemplate/technicaldataagv/1/0'})
MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm)
RETURN aas.idShort, aas.id, sm.idShort, sm.id

MATCH (instance:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:DERIVED_FROM]->(type:AssetAdministrationShell {idShort: 'MiR100_Type'})
RETURN instance.idShort, instance.id, type.idShort, type.id

MATCH (instance:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:DERIVED_FROM]->(type:AssetAdministrationShell {idShort: 'MiR250_Type'})
RETURN instance.idShort, instance.id, type.idShort, type.id

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
WHERE aas.idShort IN ['MiR100_001', 'MiR250_001', 'MiR250_002']
RETURN aas.idShort, sm.idShort, sm.id

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
WHERE aas.idShort IN ['MiR250_001', 'MiR250_002', 'MiR100_001']
RETURN aas.idShort, sm.idShort, sm.id

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
WHERE aas.idShort IN ['MiR250_001', 'MiR250_002', 'MiR100_001']
OPTIONAL MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(sc:SemanticConcept)
RETURN aas.idShort, sm.idShort, sm.id, sc.id AS templateSemanticId

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell {idShort: 'MiR250_001'})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
      -[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
RETURN sm.idShort, el.idShort, labels(el) AS types
LIMIT 20

MATCH (sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(sc:SemanticConcept)
WHERE sc.id = 'https://admin-shell.io/idta/smt/assetlocation/1/0'
MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm)
RETURN aas.idShort, sm.idShort, sm.id

MATCH (sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(sc:SemanticConcept)
RETURN DISTINCT sc.id

MATCH (sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(:SemanticConcept {id: 'https://admin-shell.io/dfki/SubmodelTemplate/FacilityInformation/1/0'})
MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm)
RETURN aas.idShort, aas.id, sm.idShort, sm.id

MATCH (sm:Submodel {idShort: 'FacilityInformation'})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
RETURN sm.idShort, el.idShort, el.value, labels(el) AS types

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell {idShort: 'MiR250_001'})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
      -[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(rel:RelationshipElement)
RETURN rel.idShort, labels(rel) AS types

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:MANAGES_ASSET]->(asset:Asset)
WHERE aas.idShort IN ['MiR250_001', 'MiR250_002', 'MiR100_001']
RETURN aas.idShort, asset.globalAssetId, asset.assetKind, asset.assetType

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
RETURN aas.idShort, sm.idShort, sm.id

MATCH (sm:Submodel {idShort: 'HierarchicalStructures'})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
RETURN sm.idShort, el.idShort, el.value, labels(el) AS types

MATCH (sm:Submodel {idShort: 'HierarchicalStructures'})-[:HAS_ELEMENT]->(parent:Entity)
WHERE parent.idShort = 'EntryNode'
MATCH (parent)-[:HAS_ELEMENT]->(child:Entity)
RETURN parent.idShort, child.idShort, labels(child) AS types

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell {idShort: 'Hall3'})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel {idShort: 'HierarchicalStructures'})
      -[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:Entity)
WHERE el.idShort IN ['MiR100_001', 'MiR250_001', 'MiR250_002']
RETURN aas.idShort, el.idShort

MATCH (sm:Submodel {idShort: 'HierarchicalStructures'})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
WHERE el.idShort = 'MiR100_001'
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(:SemanticConcept)
MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm)
RETURN aas.idShort, el.idShort

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell {idShort: 'Hall3'})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel {idShort: 'FacilityInformation'})
      -[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
RETURN el.idShort, el.value

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell {idShort: 'Hall4'})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel {idShort: 'FacilityInformation'})
      -[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
RETURN el.idShort, el.value

According to the graph data, we have **3 autonomous transport robots** (MiR - Mobile Industrial Robots):

| Robot ID | Model | Location (Hall) | Hall Primary Use |
|----------|-------|-----------------|------------------|
| **MiR100_001** | MiR100 | Hall4 | Logistics |
| **MiR250_001** | MiR250 | Hall3 | Assembly |
| **MiR250_002** | MiR250 | Hall3 | Assembly |

**Summary:**
- **Hall3 (Assembly)** contains 2 robots: MiR250_001 and MiR250_002
- **Hall4 (Logistics)** contains 1 robot: MiR100_001

The location information is stored in the HierarchicalStructures submodels of each hall, which list the assets (including robots) that are part of that facility.

result.response:
I need to find autonomous transport robots in the AAS system. Let me break this down:

1. First, I need to identify what submodel templates are used for autonomous transport robots
2. Then search the graph for assets that match this category
3. Extract their locations from the graph data

Let me start by getting the templates index to understand what templates might relate to autonomous transport robots.









I can see there's a template called "Technical Data for Automated Guided Vehicles" (idShort: "TechnicalDataAGV") which is exactly what I need for autonomous transport robots. I also see "Data Model for Asset Location" which could help with location information.

Let me search the graph for assets that have submodels with the AGV technical data template semanticId.









I found two type shells (MiR100_Type and MiR250_Type) with AGV technical data submodels. These are type shells, not the actual instance robots. I need to find the instance shells that are derived from these type shells.

Let me search for instance shells that are derived from these type shells.









I found 3 instance shells:
- MiR100_001 (derived from MiR100_Type)
- MiR250_002 (derived from MiR250_Type)
- MiR250_001 (derived from MiR250_Type)

Now I need to find the location information for these instance shells. Let me check if they have submodels related to location. I'll look for submodels with the "Data Model for Asset Location" template.









I see that the instance shells have "Nameplate" submodels but no "Data Model for Asset Location" submodels. Let me check if there's location information within the Nameplate submodel or other submodels. Let me also check if there are any other submodels I might have missed.









Each instance shell only has a Nameplate submodel. Let me check the Nameplate submodel for location information. I'll look at the elements within the Nameplate submodel to see if there's any location data.









The Nameplate submodel for MiR100_001 contains SerialNumber, DateOfManufacture, YearOfConstruction, and URIOfTheProduct - but no location information. Let me check the other instance shells as well to see if any have location data.

















None of the Nameplate submodels contain location information. Let me check if there's a "Data Model for Asset Location" submodel anywhere in the system, and also check if there are any other submodels that might contain location data.









Let me fix the query - I need to properly join the AAS to the submodel.









There are no submodels with the "Data Model for Asset Location" template in the graph. Let me check if there's any location information stored elsewhere - perhaps in the Nameplate submodel or as properties within the submodels.

Let me look at the structure of the Nameplate submodel to see if it contains location information.









The Nameplate submodel contains serial number, date of manufacture, year of construction, and URI of the product, but no location information.

Let me check if there's any location-related property in any of the submodels for these AGV instances. I'll search for properties that might contain location information.









I need to avoid using CONTAINS on idShort. Let me try a different approach - I'll list all properties and look for location-related ones by their exact names.









None of the properties contain location information. The data only includes SerialNumber, DateOfManufacture, YearOfConstruction, and URIOfTheProduct.

Let me check if there are any other submodels or elements that might contain location data. I'll also check if there's a FacilityInformation submodel that might be associated with these assets.









Each instance shell only has a Nameplate submodel. There's no location information in the current AAS data for these autonomous transport robots.

Let me also check if there are any other AGV-related instance shells that I might have missed. Let me search for all assets that have the TechnicalDataAGV submodel.









So the only assets with TechnicalDataAGV submodels are the type shells (MiR100_Type and MiR250_Type). The instance shells (MiR100_001, MiR250_001, MiR250_002) are derived from these types but don't have their own TechnicalDataAGV submodels - they only have Nameplate submodels.

Let me verify if the instance shells have any other submodels that might contain location information by checking all submodels in the entire graph for any location-related data.









I can see the semantic IDs used in the graph:
1. CapabilityDescription
2. FacilityInformation
3. Nameplate
4. HierarchicalStructures
5. TechnicalDataAGV

Let me check if there are any FacilityInformation submodels that might contain location data for the AGVs.









There are FacilityInformation submodels for Hall4 and Hall3. These might contain location information. Let me check what information is stored in these FacilityInformation submodels.









The FacilityInformation submodel for Hall4 contains PrimaryUse, Area (12000), and PowerConnection (10). Let me check Hall3 as well.









Hall3 has Area 8000 and PowerConnection 10. Now I need to find out which hall (if any) the AGVs are located in. Let me check if there's a relationship between the AGV instance shells and the hall facility information.

Let me search for any relationship between the AGV shells and the hall shells.









I see that Hall3 and Hall4 have FacilityInformation and HierarchicalStructures submodels. Let me check if there's a relationship between the AGV shells and the hall shells through the HierarchicalStructures submodel.









Let me check the HierarchicalStructures submodel more carefully to see what elements it contains.









Let me check which AAS has the HierarchicalStructures submodel and see if it relates to the AGVs.









So Hall3 and Hall4 have HierarchicalStructures submodels. Let me check if the AGV instance shells are somehow related to these halls. Let me look at the EntryNode entities in the HierarchicalStructures submodels to see if they reference the AGVs.









Excellent! I found the location information! The HierarchicalStructures submodels in Hall3 and Hall4 contain Entity nodes that represent the assets. I can see:
- MiR250_002
- MiR250_001
- MiR100_001

These are the autonomous transport robots (AGVs). Now I need to determine which hall each robot is located in. Let me check which Hall's HierarchicalStructures contains each robot.









So Hall3 contains MiR250_001 and MiR250_002. Now let me check Hall4 for MiR100_001.









I now have the complete information:

**Autonomous Transport Robots (AGVs):**

2. **MiR250_001** - Located in **Hall3**
3. **MiR250_002** - Located in **Hall3**

Let me verify this information by checking the modelType of these entities to confirm they are indeed the AGV instance shells.









Perfect! I have confirmed the location information. Let me also verify that these are indeed autonomous transport robots by checking if they have the TechnicalDataAGV submodel (through their type shells).

result.query: Which transport robot can carry more than 200 kg?
result.response:
I need to find transport robots and their load capacities. Let me start by exploring the graph structure to understand how robots and their specifications are stored.









Now let me get the templates index to understand what templates are available for finding transport robots and their specifications.









I see there's a "Technical Data for Automated Guided Vehicles" template which seems relevant for transport robots. Let me also check what submodels exist in the graph to find transport robots. Let me first query for all AAS and their assets to see what's available.









I can see several MiR (Mobile Industrial Robot) models which are transport robots: MiR100, MiR250. Let me check the submodels of these AAS to find technical data about their load capacity. Let me start with the MiR250 Type shell to see its structure.









Good, I found a MiR250_Type shell with a TechnicalDataAGV submodel. Let me also check for other transport robots and their TechnicalDataAGV submodels.









I found two Type shells: MiR100_Type and MiR250_Type. Now let me check if there are instance shells as well, and get the load capacity data from the TechnicalDataAGV submodels.









Let me also check for instance shells (non-Type) that might be derived from these type shells.









No instance shells found. Let me now explore the TechnicalDataAGV submodel structure to find the load capacity property. First, let me get the template structure.









I can see the template structure. The key property I need is `MaxLoadMass` which is under `TechnicalParameters`. Let me now query the actual data from both MiR100_Type and MiR250_Type to get their MaxLoadMass values.









Good, MiR100 has a MaxLoadMass of 100. Now let me check MiR250.









MiR250 has a MaxLoadMass of 250. Let me also check if there are any other transport robots in the system. Let me search for all AAS that have TechnicalDataAGV submodels to make sure I haven't missed any.









I have found all transport robots with TechnicalDataAGV submodels. Let me also check if there are any instance shells (non-Type) that might have their own TechnicalDataAGV submodels.









No instance shells with TechnicalDataAGV submodels found. Let me also verify by checking all AAS that have any TechnicalDataAGV submodel regardless of semanticId (in case some use a different template ID).









I have confirmed that there are only two transport robots with TechnicalDataAGV submodels:
- MiR100_Type with MaxLoadMass of 100 kg
- MiR250_Type with MaxLoadMass of 250 kg

Now I can answer the user's question: Which transport robot can carry more than 200 kg?

Based on the data:
- MiR100: 100 kg (does NOT carry more than 200 kg)
- MiR250: 250 kg (DOES carry more than 200 kg)

**Answer:** The **MiR250** transport robot can carry more than 200 kg (specifically, it has a maximum load capacity of 250 kg).

The MiR100 can only carry 100 kg, which is below the 200 kg threshold.

records.query: Which transport robot can carry more than 200 kg?

result.query: Which transport robot can carry more than 200 kg?
result.response:
I need to find transport robots in the AAS system and check their payload capacities.

Let me start by exploring the graph structure to understand how transport robots are represented and where their payload data is stored.









I see there's a "Technical Data for Automated Guided Vehicles" template which is relevant for transport robots (AGVs). Let me first explore what assets exist in the graph to find transport robots.









I can see there are `TechnicalDataAGV` submodels for `mir100` and `mir250`. These appear to be transport robots (AGVs). Let me get the template structure for TechnicalDataAGV to understand what properties it contains, particularly looking for payload capacity.









Perfect! I can see that the `TechnicalDataAGV` template contains a `MaxLoadMass` property under `TechnicalParameters`. This is what I need to check for payload capacity. Now let me query the graph to find the MaxLoadMass values for the AGVs (mir100 and mir250).









I found the MaxLoadMass values for two transport robots:
- **MiR100_Type**: 100 kg
- **MiR250_Type**: 250 kg

The question asks which transport robot can carry **more than 200 kg**. Based on this data:
- MiR100: 100 kg (does NOT exceed 200 kg)
- MiR250: 250 kg (exceeds 200 kg)

Let me also check if there are any instance shells (not just type shells) that might have different values, to be thorough.









The data shows only type shells. Let me also check if there are instance shells (which would be derived from these types) that might have their own TechnicalDataAGV submodels with potentially different values.









There are instance shells. Let me check if these instances have their own TechnicalDataAGV submodels with payload data.









The instance shells (MiR100_001, MiR250_001, MiR250_002) don't have their own TechnicalDataAGV submodels - they inherit from the type shells. So the payload capacity is defined at the type level.

**Summary of findings:**

| Transport Robot | Max Load Mass |
|-----------------|---------------|
| MiR100          | 100 kg        |
| MiR250          | 250 kg        |

**Answer:** According to the graph data, the **MiR250** transport robot can carry more than 200 kg (specifically 250 kg). The MiR100 can only carry 100 kg, which is below the 200 kg threshold.

The instance shells (MiR100_001, MiR250_001, MiR250_002) don't have their own TechnicalDataAGV submodels - they inherit from the type shells. So the payload capacity is defined at the type level.

**Summary of findings:**

|-----------------|---------------|

| MiR250          | 250 kg        |

**Answer:** According to the graph data, the **MiR250** transport robot can carry more than 200 kg (specifically 250 kg). The MiR100 can only carry 100 kg, which is below the 200 kg threshold.

records.query: Which transport robot can carry more than 200 kg?

result.query: Which transport robot can carry more than 200 kg?
result.response:
I need to find transport robots in the AAS system and check their payload capacities. Let me start by exploring the graph structure to understand how robots and their specifications are stored.









Now let me check the templates index to understand what templates are available, particularly for technical specifications or capabilities that might contain payload information.









I see there's a "Technical Data for Automated Guided Vehicles" template which is relevant for transport robots (AGVs). Let me first query the graph to find all assets and their submodels to identify transport robots.









Good, I can see there are MiR (Mobile Industrial Robot) assets which are transport robots. Let me check the submodels of these MiR assets to find technical data about their payload capacity. I'll start by checking the submodels of the MiR100 and MiR250 assets.









Good, I found Type AAS shells for MiR100 and MiR250 with TechnicalDataAGV submodels. Now let me check if there are instance shells as well, and then query the TechnicalDataAGV submodels for payload information.









Now let me explore the TechnicalDataAGV submodel structure to find payload information. First, let me get the template structure for TechnicalDataAGV.









I can see the template structure. The key property for payload is `MaxLoadMass` under `TechnicalParameters`. Now let me query the actual data from both MiR100 and MiR250 TechnicalDataAGV submodels to get the MaxLoadMass values.









Perfect! I found the payload data:
- MiR100_Type: MaxLoadMass = 100 kg
- MiR250_Type: MaxLoadMass = 250 kg

The question asks which transport robot can carry more than 200 kg. Based on this data:
- MiR100 carries 100 kg (does NOT meet the >200 kg requirement)
- MiR250 carries 250 kg (MEETS the >200 kg requirement)

Let me also check if there are any instance shells (not just type shells) to provide complete information about available robots.









Let me fix the query - Neo4j uses different syntax for pattern matching.









Good, I've confirmed the type shells. Now let me also check if there are instance shells for MiR robots (not just type shells).









The instance shells don't have their own TechnicalDataAGV submodels - they likely derive from the type shells. Let me verify by checking what submodels the instance shells have.









The instance shells only have Nameplate submodels, not TechnicalDataAGV. They likely inherit technical data from their type shells via the DERIVED_FROM relationship. Let me verify this structure.









Perfect! The instance shells derive from type shells, so they inherit the technical data. Now I have all the information needed to answer the question.

**Summary of findings:**
- MiR100_Type: MaxLoadMass = 100 kg
- MiR250_Type: MaxLoadMass = 250 kg

The question asks which transport robot can carry **more than 200 kg**.

**Answer:** Only the **MiR250** (Mobile Industrial Robot 250) can carry more than 200 kg, with a maximum load capacity of 250 kg. The MiR100 can only carry 100 kg, which is below the 200 kg threshold.

According to the graph data from the TechnicalDataAGV submodel, the MiR250_Type has a MaxLoadMass of 250 kg, while the MiR100_Type has a MaxLoadMass of 100 kg.
result.final_answer:

**Summary of findings:**

- MiR250_Type: MaxLoadMass = 250 kg

**Answer:** Only the **MiR250** (Mobile Industrial Robot 250) can carry more than 200 kg, with a maximum load capacity of 250 kg. The MiR100 can only carry 100 kg, which is below the 200 kg threshold.

According to the graph data from the TechnicalDataAGV submodel, the MiR250_Type has a MaxLoadMass of 250 kg, while the MiR100_Type has a MaxLoadMass of 100 kg.
